channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: EU investigates TikTok over Romanian presidential election safeguards
item.description: LONDON (AP) — European Union regulators said Tuesday they're investigating whether TikTok breached the bloc's digital rulebook by failing to deal with risks to Romania's presidential election, which has been thrown into turmoil over allegations of electoral violations and Russian meddling.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Evolution of Holiday Wish Lists
item.description: From handwritten notes to curated registries, the way we approach holiday wish lists has changed dramatically over the years. Discover how Gen Z is elevating the wish-list game and what it means for gift-giving traditions.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Samsung’s latest Care+ upgrade makes AppleCare+ look like bad value
item.description: Samsung will soon offer free repairs for cracks and chips in smartphone screens as part of its highest-tier Care Plus protection plan, ditching the prior $29 charge.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia's Next-Gen Desktop Graphics Cards Leaked: RTX 5060 Models Incoming?
item.description: Nvidia's next-gen desktop graphics cards have been the subject of a flood of leaks, but this time we're hearing about the RTX 5060 models that'll sit at the bottom of the Blackwell GeForce stack.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 5 reasons why 2025 will be the year of OpenTelemetry
item.description: Some open-source projects are spectacularly successful and become standard components of the IT infrastructure. Take Linux or Kubernetes, for example.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: CD Projekt Red hints at Gwent returning in The Witcher 4 so there goes literally all of my spare time
item.description: Yep, Gwent is probably coming back in The Witcher 4 and I don't know whether to be excited or scared.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: US moves to tighten restrictions on China Telecom amid security fears
item.description: The US Commerce Department is intensifying its actions against China Telecom’s US unit over concerns that its cloud and internet services could be used to funnel American data to Beijing, according to a Reuters report.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Ozy Media founder Carlos Watson sentenced to 10 years
item.description: Carlos Watson, founder of the defunct digital media company Ozy Media, was sentenced to 10 years in prison for lying to investors about the startup's finances.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Corps of Engineers will look to save 150-year-old lighthouse from crumbling into the Hudson River
item.description: Efforts to save an imperiled 150-year-old lighthouse in New York are getting a boost with federal officials announcing new funding to study ways to protect the structure.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Senate report alleges Amazon rejected warehouse safety recommendations due to productivity concerns
item.description: At least two internal Amazon studies found a link between how quickly the online retailer's warehouse workers perform tasks and workplace injuries, but the company rejected many safety recommendations out of concern the proposed changes might reduce productivity, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: These are the most downloaded iPhone and iPad apps of 2024 – including a controversial top free choice
item.description: Apple has revealed the most popular apps and games for iPhone and iPad–and there are a few surprises.
item.dc:creator: alexblake.techradar@gmail.com (Alex Blake), TechRadar

item.title: Former Google CEO: A time will come to consider 'unplugging' AI system
item.description: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt said that the power of artificial intelligence (AI) can reach a “dangerous” point in the future and that humanity should be ready to step away from it should the time come.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: The Rise of the IT Generalist: How Automation is Changing the Face of IT Teams
item.description: As technology evolves toward a frictionless IT, IT teams are automating processes to quickly adopt new technologies and SaaS apps, leading to the rise of the IT generalist.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Infosys co-founder once again claims 70-hour work weeks are "necessary" and will create jobs
item.description: Indian billionaire Narayana Murthy still believes 70-hour work weeks are important for India’s economic growth.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Eddie AI is one of the best video editing tools out there
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: How to upgrade to the Hyte Y70 PC case to give your parts more breathing room
item.description: The Hyte Y70 is a stylish PC case that will help PC gamers get the most out of their graphics cards and other high-end parts
item.dc:creator: Gieson Cacho, The Mercury News

item.title: How to delete multiple emails at once on Android
item.description: Cut through all the digital clutter and delete multiple emails from your Android simultaneously. Kurt the CyberGuy explains how it's done.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple CEO visits Mar-a-Lago, joining list of tech execs seemingly courting Trump
item.description: Apple CEO Tim Cook visited Mar-a-Lago on Friday, joining a growing list of tech executives that are seemingly courting President-elect Trump ahead of his return to the White House. The president-elect and Cook had dinner at Trump's Florida resort in West Palm Beach, multiple outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Lawmakers tell Apple, Google to prepare for TikTok ban
item.description: TikTok now plans to appeal to the Supreme Court and has asked the D.C. Circuit to put the law on hold while it appeals the case.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Chatbot's deadly prompt
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese phone hack victims haven't been notified: Report
item.description: AT&T and Verizon have reportedly not informed some hacking victims that they were specifically targeted.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Sophia, a famous robot and global icon of AI, wins hearts at Zimbabwe's innovation fair
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — From answering questions from Cabinet ministers, academics and students on climate change, substance abuse and the law to children’s inquiries about her “birth” and links to God and being described as a talkative feminist, Sophia, the world-famous robot won hearts at an innov
item.dc:creator: BY FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Google’s Agentspace will put AI agents in the hands of workers
item.description: Google has unveiled an AI agent builder tool designed to automate repititive tasks and help workers find information held across their organization faster. AI agents have become a major focus for software vendors in recent months, including Atlassian, Microsoft, Salesforce, and numerous others.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft introduces Phi-4, an AI model for advanced reasoning tasks
item.description: Microsoft has announced Phi-4 — a new AI model with 14 billion parameters — designed for complex reasoning tasks, including mathematics. Phi-4 excels in areas such as STEM question-answering and advanced problem-solving, surpassing similar models in performance.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Python AI library hack that didn’t hack Python
item.description: Guess what’s both one of the fastest-growing and most popular languages of 2024? (Hint: It’s not Java.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump offers support for dockworkers union by saying ports shouldn’t install more automated systems
item.description: The International Longshoremen’s Association has until Jan. 15 to negotiate a new contract with the U.S. Maritime Alliance, which represents ports and shipping companies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Enterprise buyer’s guide: How to choose videoconferencing software
item.description: When most businesspeople think of videoconferencing software, the first thing that comes to mind is probably Microsoft Teams or Zoom, but there are many other choices, each with its own strengths.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google’s AI coding entry with Jules signals tougher competition in coding tools
item.description: Google has made a significant entry into the competitive AI coding tools market with Jules, an experimental coding assistant aimed at enhancing developer workflows and challenging established tools like GitHub Copilot and Amazon Q Developer.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google claims breakthrough with Willow quantum computing chip but no real-world use yet
item.description: Google’s announcement on Monday of its new quantum chip, called Willow, was full of eye-catching statements. For starters, Willow took less than a minute to perform a benchmark computation that would take one of today’s fastest supercomputers 10 septillion years to do.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: AI app helps you turn anything into LEGO models
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft to launch zero water consumption cooling for future data centers
item.description: Microsoft has introduced a new design for data centers to optimize artificial intelligence (AI) workloads, implementing a cooling system that it claims will consume zero water.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: NDAA to give DoD components more flexibility to procure cyber products
item.description: A provision that made it into the compromise legislation will give the Defense Department components more authority to purchase alternative cyber products. The post NDAA to give DoD components more flexibility to procure cyber products first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Fortnite players 'tricked' into unwanted purchases are starting to get refunds. Here's how to apply
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. consumers who were “tricked” into purchases they didn't want from Fortnite maker Epic Games are now starting to receive refund checks, the Federal Trade Commission said this week.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A hardwired TSA looks to the future with 5G implementation
item.description: At TSA most of their critical network infrastructure is hardwired. That includes checkpoint equipment and computers operated by the TSOs. The post A hardwired TSA looks to the future with 5G implementation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network
